title: Fraction Equality and Ordering
prompt_md:
## Goal

Make a `Fraction` class comparable by numeric value using the C++20 three-way comparison operator. Implement `operator<=>` (returning `std::strong_ordering`) and `operator==`. Fractions are compared by cross-multiplication: `a/b` versus `c/d` is the comparison of `a*d` versus `c*b` (all denominators are positive). A user-provided `<=>` does not synthesize `==`, so you must define `==` as well.

## Input

- Line 1: two integers `n1 d1` (first fraction).
- Line 2: two integers `n2 d2` (second fraction).

## Output

- Line 1: `equal` if the fractions are numerically equal, else `not_equal`.
- Line 2: `less` if fraction1 < fraction2, `greater` if fraction1 > fraction2, else `equivalent`.
- Line 3: the result of `fraction1 <= fraction2` as `true`/`false`.

The provided `main` uses your operators to produce all three lines. Include `<compare>`.

title: Student Record Sorting with Default Spaceship
prompt_md:
## Goal

Make a `Student` class comparable with a single line of code: a **defaulted** `operator<=>`. A defaulted spaceship compares members in declaration order and generates all six relational operators (and `==`) for free. Then the provided `main` sorts three students ascending using the generated `operator<`.

## Input

- Three lines, each an integer `grade` (0-100) followed by a string `name` (no spaces).

## Output

- Three lines of `grade name`, sorted ascending. Because the members are declared as `m_grade` then `m_name`, ties on grade break by name lexicographically.

Use `auto operator<=>(const Student&) const = default;` -- do not write custom comparison logic. A `print()` member is provided. Include `<compare>`.

test_cases.label: Tie broken by name across grades
